problems.title: Complete PFAS Degradation Pathway
problems.tagline: Break the C-F bond in 'forever chemicals' at scale.
problems.summary: Develop a scalable pathway for complete mineralization of PFAS compounds.
problems.description: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) resist natural degradation due to the exceptional strength of the C-F bond (~485 kJ/mol). Recent advances — UV/sulfite photolysis, electrochemical boron-doped diamond anodes, supercritical water oxidation, and the 2024 Purdue low-temperature defluorination — demonstrate laboratory-scale mineralization. A unified, scalable, energy-efficient pathway is within reach.
problems.abstract: This project targets complete PFAS mineralization via integrated reaction engineering. We will (1) benchmark UV-sulfite, electrochemical, and SCWO pathways across representative PFAS classes using 19F NMR and total organic fluorine quantitation, (2) develop hybrid electrochemical-photocatalytic reactors optimizing energy per C-F cleaved, and (3) apply machine learning to map substrate-reactor performance relationships. A successful pipeline would address one of the most urgent environmental chemistry challenges.

problems.title: Recyclable Thermoset Polymers
problems.tagline: Thermosets that can be reshaped and recycled like thermoplastics.
problems.summary: Design high-performance thermosets with fully reversible crosslinks.
problems.description: Traditional thermosets are permanently crosslinked and non-recyclable. Vitrimers, introduced by Leibler in 2011, use dynamic covalent bonds (transesterification, disulfide exchange, Diels-Alder) to enable topology rearrangement above a transition temperature. Commercial deployment is emerging but performance gaps vs conventional thermosets remain.
problems.abstract: We target high-performance recyclable thermosets through (1) systematic screening of dynamic covalent chemistries for balanced thermomechanical and exchange kinetics, (2) design of catalyst-free vitrimers leveraging neighboring group participation, and (3) closed-loop life-cycle analysis of recycling efficiency. Commercial-grade recyclable thermosets would transform the polymer industry.

problems.title: Direct Air Capture Below $100/ton CO₂
problems.tagline: Engineer DAC systems economically competitive with carbon markets.
problems.summary: Reduce direct air capture cost below $100 per tonne of CO₂.
problems.description: Climeworks and Carbon Engineering operate commercial DAC at costs between $600 and $1000/ton. Reducing cost to $100/ton requires improvements in sorbent capacity, regeneration energy, contactor design, and renewable integration. This is primarily an engineering challenge with clear optimization paths.
problems.abstract: This project targets $100/ton DAC by (1) developing amine-functionalized MOF sorbents with higher CO₂ capacity per mole of amine, (2) optimizing low-temperature regeneration via pressure/humidity swing, and (3) techno-economic analysis of wind/solar integration at gigatonne scale. Achieving the target would make DAC deployment economically feasible at climate-relevant scales.

problems.title: Stable Perovskite Solar Cells (>25 yr)
problems.tagline: Extend perovskite PV lifetime to match silicon's 25-year warranty.
problems.summary: Achieve 25+ year operational stability in high-efficiency perovskite photovoltaics.
problems.description: Hybrid organic-inorganic perovskites have reached 26% efficiency but degrade under heat, moisture, and UV. 2D/3D heterostructures, inorganic caps, and robust encapsulation are narrowing the stability gap. Commercial deployment requires matching silicon's 25-year warranty.
problems.abstract: We pursue perovskite stability via (1) systematic 2D/3D passivation strategies with accelerated ISOS-L testing, (2) ion-migration suppression through grain boundary engineering, and (3) encapsulation chemistry for hermetic long-term protection. Commercial-grade stability enables transformative solar deployment.

problems.title: Complete Metabolomics of Human Blood
problems.tagline: Identify every metabolite circulating in human blood.
problems.summary: Annotate the ~60% of blood metabolites currently classified as unknown.
problems.description: Current blood metabolomics identifies ~40% of detected features; the rest are 'dark matter' awaiting structural assignment. Ion mobility MS, high-resolution NMR, and machine-learning annotation tools are rapidly closing the gap, enabling disease biomarker discovery.
problems.abstract: This project targets complete blood metabolome annotation via (1) multimodal LC-IM-MS and NMR acquisition across population cohorts, (2) ML-assisted structural assignment leveraging recent libraries, and (3) integration with pathway databases for biological interpretation. Completion would revolutionize precision medicine.

problems.title: Predictive Retrosynthesis AI
problems.tagline: Build an AlphaFold-equivalent for chemical synthesis planning.
problems.summary: Achieve expert-level accuracy in multi-step retrosynthetic planning.
problems.description: ASKCOS, Synthia, and Chemputer demonstrate AI-assisted retrosynthesis but trail expert chemists on complex natural products. Transformer architectures trained on reaction databases show rapid progress. A fully predictive tool would transform medicinal chemistry and drug discovery.
problems.abstract: We pursue retrosynthesis AI by (1) training large reaction-transformer models on curated and patent-mined datasets, (2) integrating reaction mechanism constraints via graph neural networks, and (3) closed-loop evaluation with automated synthesis platforms. Expert-level retrosynthesis AI would democratize complex molecular synthesis.

problems.title: Solid-State Battery Electrolyte
problems.tagline: Eliminate flammable liquid electrolytes with solid-state alternatives.
problems.summary: Solve interfacial resistance in solid-state lithium battery electrolytes.
problems.description: Sulfide (Li₆PS₅Cl), oxide (LLZO), and polymer solid electrolytes offer safer, higher-energy batteries but suffer from high interfacial resistance with electrodes. Samsung and Toyota are scaling production. Interfacial engineering is the key bottleneck.
problems.abstract: This project addresses solid-state battery electrolytes through (1) atomically-resolved interfacial characterization using cryo-EM and operando TEM, (2) design of interlayer chemistries minimizing resistance, and (3) scalable coating processes for commercial implementation. Success enables safer, higher-energy batteries.

problems.title: Non-Precious Metal Fuel Cell Catalysts
problems.tagline: Replace platinum in PEM fuel cells with earth-abundant catalysts.
problems.summary: Develop durable Fe-N-C or Co-N-C catalysts matching platinum performance.
problems.description: Fe-N-C and Co-N-C single-atom catalysts are approaching platinum in ORR activity. Durability remains 3-5× lower. Atomic-scale understanding of active site degradation will close the gap and eliminate the primary PEM fuel cell cost driver.
problems.abstract: We target non-precious fuel cell catalysts via (1) operando X-ray absorption and Mössbauer characterization of Fe-N-C active sites, (2) DFT-guided site engineering for durability, and (3) MEA-scale testing at commercial loadings. Eliminating Pt would make hydrogen fuel cells cost-competitive.

problems.title: Complete Annotation of the Chemical Universe
problems.tagline: Systematically characterize every synthesizable small molecule.
problems.summary: Annotate properties of all ~166 billion drug-like molecules in GDB-17.
problems.description: The GDB-17 database enumerates 166 billion possible molecules up to 17 heavy atoms. Virtual screening and ML prediction allow systematic characterization of subsets for drug-likeness, synthesizability, and biological activity. Complete annotation is becoming tractable.
problems.abstract: This project pursues systematic chemical space annotation via (1) ML-based property prediction at GDB-17 scale with active-learning sampling, (2) synthesizability scoring via retrosynthesis AI, and (3) public release of annotated databases. Completion accelerates drug discovery across the pharmaceutical industry.

problems.title: Selective C-H Functionalization (General)
problems.tagline: Activate any C-H bond selectively without directing groups.
problems.summary: Develop general, directing-group-free selective C-H activation.
problems.description: Directed C-H activation (Pd, Ru, Rh) works for specific substrate classes. General, site-selective C-H functionalization without directing groups remains elusive. Electrochemical, photoredox, and radical approaches show promise for late-stage diversification.
problems.abstract: We address general C-H functionalization via (1) photoredox and HAT-based radical strategies for non-activated C-H bonds, (2) outer-sphere catalyst designs avoiding substrate coordination, and (3) machine-learning-guided selectivity prediction. Progress would transform retrosynthetic planning.

problems.title: Artificial Leaf (Solar-to-Fuel >10%)
problems.tagline: Achieve 10%+ solar-to-fuel efficiency in integrated water-splitting devices.
problems.summary: Build integrated photoelectrochemical systems above 10% solar-to-H₂ efficiency.
problems.description: Nocera's cobalt catalyst and subsequent artificial leaf designs demonstrate water splitting with modest efficiency. Integration of light absorption, water oxidation, and proton reduction within a single stable device is the remaining challenge. 10% STH is the threshold for economic viability.
problems.abstract: This project targets 10% solar-to-fuel via (1) earth-abundant OER catalyst development matching IrO₂ activity, (2) semiconductor photoelectrode engineering for stability, and (3) integrated device optimization via multiphysics modeling. Commercial-grade artificial leaves would close the solar-fuel gap.

problems.title: Room-Temperature Nitrogen Fixation
problems.tagline: Replace Haber-Bosch with ambient-condition N₂ reduction.
problems.summary: Develop catalytic N₂ → NH₃ at ambient temperature and pressure.
problems.description: Haber-Bosch operates at 450°C/200 atm, consuming ~2% of global energy. Nitrogenase enzymes achieve the same transformation at ambient conditions via a MoFe cofactor. Synthetic molybdenum and iron complexes are approaching catalytic activity but turnover and efficiency remain limited.
problems.abstract: We pursue RT nitrogen fixation through (1) rational design of MoFe and Fe-based catalysts inspired by nitrogenase, (2) electrochemical N₂ reduction with suppressed HER selectivity, and (3) mechanistic studies via operando spectroscopy. Success would revolutionize fertilizer production and global energy use.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of Water
problems.tagline: Resolve water's anomalous thermodynamic and structural properties.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theory explaining water's 70+ anomalies.
problems.description: Water exhibits density maximum at 4°C, heat capacity maximum, 15+ ice phases, and a debated liquid-liquid critical point. Two-liquid models and nuclear quantum effects capture partial pictures but a unified theory is lacking. Progress would impact biology, catalysis, and atmospheric chemistry.
problems.abstract: This project targets a unified water theory via (1) ab initio MD simulations including nuclear quantum effects across full T-P range, (2) experimental validation of the proposed liquid-liquid critical point, and (3) theoretical framework reconciling structural and dynamical anomalies. A unified picture impacts every aqueous science.

problems.title: Programmable Molecular Machines
problems.tagline: Extract useful work from nanoscale molecular motors.
problems.summary: Build molecular machines performing programmable macroscopic work.
problems.description: Stoddart, Sauvage, and Feringa's Nobel-winning work established molecular motors, shuttles, and pumps. Extracting useful macroscopic work requires collective action of trillions of machines. Programmability and energy efficiency remain open.
problems.abstract: We pursue programmable molecular machines via (1) collective motor arrays in ordered matrices, (2) chemical-fuel-driven motors for autonomous operation, and (3) scalable assembly protocols. Useful work output would realize a key vision of nanotechnology.

problems.title: Efficient Palytoxin Total Synthesis
problems.tagline: Shorten palytoxin's 65-step synthesis to under 20 steps.
problems.summary: Dramatically shorten total synthesis of palytoxin using modern methods.
problems.description: Kishi's 1994 palytoxin synthesis (over 65 steps) remains a landmark in organic chemistry. Modern retrosynthetic AI, biocatalysis, and catalytic late-stage functionalization could shorten it dramatically. This is a benchmark problem for synthesis efficiency.
problems.abstract: We target efficient palytoxin synthesis via (1) biocatalytic cascades for stereoselective polyol construction, (2) retrosynthetic AI-guided disconnections, and (3) convergent assembly via late-stage glycosylation. A sub-20-step route would set a new standard for complex molecule synthesis.

problems.title: Autonomous Self-Healing Materials
problems.tagline: Materials that fully recover after repeated damage.
problems.summary: Develop materials with fast, repeated, autonomous structural self-healing.
problems.description: Microencapsulated healants and vascular networks provide limited self-healing. Repeated, fast, and fully autonomous healing of structural polymers remains elusive. Dynamic bonds and biomimetic designs are promising.
problems.abstract: This project targets autonomous self-healing via (1) multi-scale dynamic bond chemistries combining covalent and non-covalent exchange, (2) vascular network designs with long-term integrity, and (3) biomimetic healing inspired by vertebrate skin. True autonomous healing transforms materials durability.

problems.title: Electrochemical CO₂ to Multi-Carbon Products
problems.tagline: Selectively convert CO₂ to ethanol or propanol at high Faradaic efficiency.
problems.summary: Achieve selective CO₂ electroreduction to multi-carbon products above 70% FE.
problems.description: CO₂ electroreduction to CO and formate is well-established. Multi-carbon products (ethylene, ethanol, propanol) are generally achieved only at modest selectivity on copper catalysts. Atomic-level understanding of Cu structure-activity relationships is key.
problems.abstract: We target CO₂ electroreduction to C2+ via (1) Cu nanoparticle engineering for specific facets and grain boundaries, (2) operando XAS and Raman tracking of reaction intermediates, and (3) tandem catalysis combining CO₂ activation with C-C coupling. Progress would enable carbon-neutral chemical production.

problems.title: Sequence-Defined Polymer Synthesis
problems.tagline: Control vinyl polymer sequence with DNA-level precision.
problems.summary: Achieve monomer sequence control in synthetic vinyl polymers.
problems.description: DNA and peptide synthesis achieves perfect sequence control, but vinyl polymerization (radical, anionic) does not. Sequence control enables precise structure-property relationships and biological-mimetic functions. Iterative approaches and living polymerization are key strategies.
problems.abstract: This project targets sequence-defined polymers via (1) iterative single-monomer addition with living polymerization, (2) templated polymerization directing monomer insertion, and (3) solid-phase synthesis analogous to peptide chemistry. Success unlocks a new class of programmable materials.

problems.title: Understanding the Glass Transition
problems.tagline: Resolve the nature of the glass transition in supercooled liquids.
problems.summary: Identify the underlying mechanism of the glass transition.
problems.description: Adam-Gibbs, RFOT, and mode-coupling theories offer competing pictures of the glass transition. Kauzmann paradox and dynamic heterogeneity remain unresolved. This problem bridges chemistry and condensed matter physics.
problems.abstract: We pursue glass transition theory via (1) swap Monte Carlo simulations reaching experimental supercooling timescales, (2) replica theory validation of RFOT predictions, and (3) machine-learning identification of structural order parameters. Resolution settles a 70-year-old puzzle.

problems.title: Industrial-Scale Enzymatic Plastic Degradation
problems.tagline: Scale PETase-like enzymes to industrial plastic recycling.
problems.summary: Engineer enzymes to degrade PET, PE, and PP at industrial scale.
problems.description: PETase (2016) and FAST-PETase variants degrade PET efficiently in lab settings. Industrial scaling, broader substrate scope (polyethylene, polypropylene), and economic viability are the remaining challenges. This would transform plastic waste management.
problems.abstract: This project targets industrial enzymatic plastic degradation via (1) directed evolution of PET-degrading enzymes for temperature and substrate robustness, (2) discovery of PE/PP-active enzymes via metagenomics, and (3) bioreactor design for scalable deployment. Industrial success addresses plastic waste at source.

problems.title: Asymmetric Catalysis of Quaternary Stereocenters
problems.tagline: Achieve general enantioselective quaternary carbon formation.
problems.summary: Develop general asymmetric methods for quaternary stereocenter construction.
problems.description: Quaternary stereocenters are challenging due to steric congestion. Pd- and NHC-catalyzed asymmetric allylic alkylation provide specific solutions. General methods applicable across substrate classes remain elusive, limiting complex molecule synthesis.
problems.abstract: We pursue general quaternary stereocenter catalysis via (1) chiral Lewis acid and organocatalysts engineered for sterically demanding substrates, (2) photoredox-generated radical pairings, and (3) enzyme-inspired active-site designs. Progress would unlock natural product synthesis targets.

problems.title: Single-Molecule Electronics
problems.tagline: Build reliable room-temperature single-molecule electronic devices.
problems.summary: Achieve reproducible single-molecule transistors and rectifiers.
problems.description: Molecular wires, rectifiers, and switches have been demonstrated in break-junction experiments. Reproducibility, contact resistance, and room-temperature operation remain barriers. Advancement would enable molecular-scale electronics.
problems.abstract: This project targets single-molecule electronics via (1) designed anchor groups for reproducible metal-molecule contacts, (2) gated break-junction experiments enabling transistor action, and (3) scalable addressable arrays. Reliable single-molecule devices realize a vision of molecular electronics.

problems.title: MOF Water Harvesting
problems.tagline: Harvest drinking water from desert air with metal-organic frameworks.
problems.summary: Optimize MOFs for high-capacity, stable atmospheric water harvesting.
problems.description: MOF-303 harvests water from desert air at low humidity. Capacity, cycling stability, and cost must improve for deployment to arid populations. This application showcases MOFs' potential for humanitarian impact.
problems.abstract: We advance MOF water harvesting via (1) hydrolytically-stable MOF design principles, (2) optimization of isotherm shape for low-humidity capture, and (3) modular device engineering for field deployment. Scaled deployment could provide water to billions in arid regions.

problems.title: Homogeneous Catalysis Mechanisms
problems.tagline: Quantitatively predict homogeneous catalyst kinetics from first principles.
problems.summary: Achieve chemical-accuracy prediction of homogeneous catalytic cycles.
problems.description: DFT often fails to predict homogeneous catalyst barriers within chemical accuracy. Operando spectroscopy and microkinetic modeling narrow the gap. A predictive framework for catalyst design is the ultimate goal.
problems.abstract: This project advances homogeneous catalysis mechanism prediction via (1) benchmarking DFT functionals against coupled-cluster reference for transition states, (2) operando XAS and NMR for cycle validation, and (3) microkinetic-Bayesian integration. Predictive capability transforms catalyst design.

problems.title: Accurate Crystal Structure Prediction
problems.tagline: Predict crystal structures of complex molecules reliably.
problems.summary: Achieve reliable crystal structure prediction for molecules >100 atoms.
problems.description: Crystal structure prediction (CSP) remains unreliable for large molecules and polymorphs. The 1998 ritonavir disaster (appearance of a new, less bioavailable polymorph) exemplifies the pharmaceutical risk. Machine learning is rapidly improving CSP accuracy.
problems.abstract: We target reliable CSP via (1) ML-accelerated sampling of polymorphic energy landscapes, (2) accurate van der Waals functionals for crystal energies, and (3) automated polymorph screening pipelines. Reliable CSP would prevent pharmaceutical polymorph surprises.

problems.title: General Asymmetric C-C Bond Formation
problems.tagline: A universal chiral catalyst for any C-C disconnection.
problems.summary: Develop universally applicable asymmetric C-C bond-forming catalysts.
problems.description: Many specific asymmetric C-C bond formations (cross-coupling, conjugate addition, aldol) are well-developed. A universal chiral catalyst applicable across substrate classes remains a holy grail of organic chemistry.
problems.abstract: This project pursues universal asymmetric catalysis via (1) modular chiral catalyst libraries designed for substrate-tolerance, (2) high-throughput screening with ML prediction, and (3) mechanistic understanding of selectivity origins. Progress would transform enantioselective synthesis.

problems.title: Complete Artificial Photosynthesis
problems.tagline: Match biological photosynthesis in an integrated artificial system.
problems.summary: Integrate water oxidation, CO₂ fixation, and fuel storage in a single device.
problems.description: Biological photosynthesis integrates water oxidation, proton reduction, CO₂ fixation, and biomass storage with high efficiency. No artificial system matches this integration. Individual components (OER, HER, CO₂R) work but integration is a major challenge.
problems.abstract: We pursue complete artificial photosynthesis through (1) integrated photoelectrochemical stacks with OER/HER/CO₂R modules, (2) biomimetic electron relay design, and (3) closed-loop fuel storage and release. Matching biology would transform renewable fuel production.

problems.title: Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Prediction
problems.tagline: Predict binding affinity to within 1 kcal/mol.
problems.summary: Achieve chemical-accuracy protein-ligand binding affinity prediction.
problems.description: Free energy perturbation methods approach ~1 kcal/mol accuracy on selected systems but reliability is inconsistent. Drug discovery demands systematic chemical accuracy for hit-to-lead optimization. This is one of the central computational chemistry challenges.
problems.abstract: This project targets FEP chemical accuracy via (1) systematic force field improvements for protein-ligand systems, (2) enhanced sampling of slow conformational changes, and (3) ML-corrected DFT/MM scoring. Reliable binding prediction would transform drug discovery.

problems.title: Arbitrary Molecular Knot Synthesis
problems.tagline: Synthesize molecular knots of any topological type.
problems.summary: Develop general methods for synthesizing arbitrary molecular knots.
problems.description: Sauvage's 1989 trefoil knot and Leigh's 8₁₉ knot (2017) demonstrate increasing topological complexity. General methods for arbitrary knot types and links remain elusive. Topological chemistry has potential applications in materials and mechanical metamaterials.
problems.abstract: We pursue general molecular knot synthesis via (1) metal-template approaches for higher-order knots, (2) dynamic combinatorial assembly of topologically complex structures, and (3) computer-aided knot topology design. Progress would establish topological chemistry as a viable field.

problems.title: Room-Temperature Superconducting Material
problems.tagline: Discover an ambient-pressure room-temperature superconductor.
problems.summary: Synthesize a confirmed ambient-condition room-temperature superconductor.
problems.description: LaH₁₀ exhibits superconductivity near 250 K under 170 GPa. No confirmed ambient-pressure RT superconductor exists. The ASRP BCS ζ-spectrum and quantum metric framework provide theoretical guidance for predicting candidate materials, directly linking to ASRP's physics program on room-temperature superconductivity theory.
problems.abstract: This project pursues ambient-pressure RT superconductivity via (1) ASRP BCS ζ-spectrum screening of candidate hydrides, nickelates, and kagome metals, (2) high-throughput synthesis and magnetotransport characterization, and (3) theoretical validation against quantum metric bounds on Tc. Success would realize a transformative technology while validating the ASRP superconductivity framework.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of Enzyme Catalysis
problems.tagline: Explain enzymatic rate enhancements from first principles.
problems.summary: Provide unified first-principles explanation of enzyme rate enhancements.
problems.description: Enzymes accelerate reactions by factors up to 10¹⁷. Transition state stabilization, preorganization, dynamics, and quantum tunneling all contribute. A unified quantitative framework predicting catalytic efficiency from structure remains incomplete.
problems.abstract: We pursue unified enzyme catalysis theory via (1) QM/MM simulations integrating electrostatic preorganization and dynamics, (2) isotope-effect studies discriminating tunneling contributions, and (3) de novo enzyme design tests of theoretical predictions. Progress unlocks rational enzyme engineering.

problems.title: Programmable Universal Synthesizer
problems.tagline: Fully automated synthesis of arbitrary molecules.
problems.summary: Develop a universal automated synthesis platform for arbitrary target molecules.
problems.description: Cronin's Chemputer and flow chemistry platforms have demonstrated automated synthesis of specific molecules. Full automation for arbitrary targets requires integration of retrosynthesis AI, robotic execution, and autonomous optimization.
problems.abstract: This project pursues the universal synthesizer via (1) integration of retrosynthesis AI with robotic platforms, (2) closed-loop optimization using ML-driven condition screening, and (3) modular chemistry repositories covering diverse transformations. Success democratizes chemical synthesis.

problems.title: Non-Equilibrium Self-Assembly
problems.tagline: Design dissipative chemical structures that persist out of equilibrium.
problems.summary: Develop fuel-driven non-equilibrium self-assembled structures.
problems.description: Biological systems maintain order by dissipating energy. Synthetic dissipative self-assembly (van Esch, Hermans, Boekhoven) demonstrates fuel-driven structures but complexity lags biology. Sustained non-equilibrium operation is a frontier.
problems.abstract: We pursue non-equilibrium self-assembly via (1) chemical fuel cycles with tunable dissipation rates, (2) design of hierarchical dissipative materials, and (3) mathematical frameworks for non-equilibrium stability. Progress brings chemistry closer to life-like behavior.

problems.title: Complete Solvation Theory
problems.tagline: Develop quantitative theory of solvation effects on reactivity.
problems.summary: Unify implicit and explicit solvation models for reactive chemistry.
problems.description: Implicit solvation (PCM, SMD) captures bulk effects but misses specific interactions. Explicit solvation is computationally expensive. Marcus theory handles electron transfer. A universal framework for solvation effects on reactivity remains elusive.
problems.abstract: This project pursues unified solvation theory via (1) hybrid explicit-implicit methods with dynamic selection, (2) ML-corrected dielectric continuum models, and (3) benchmark datasets across diverse reaction types. Progress enables accurate solution-phase chemistry prediction.

problems.title: Practical Electrochemical Ammonia Synthesis
problems.tagline: Efficient direct electrochemical N₂ to NH₃.
problems.summary: Achieve high-efficiency electrochemical ammonia synthesis at ambient conditions.
problems.description: Lithium-mediated nitrogen reduction achieves ammonia synthesis but at low efficiency. Direct N₂ + H₂O → NH₃ at ambient conditions remains elusive. This would revolutionize fertilizer production with renewable electricity.
problems.abstract: We target practical electrochemical ammonia via (1) catalyst design suppressing HER selectivity, (2) membrane and reactor optimization for high Faradaic efficiency, and (3) renewable electricity integration. Success enables distributed ammonia production.

problems.title: Rational Design of Allosteric Drugs
problems.tagline: Predict and exploit cryptic allosteric sites for drug design.
problems.summary: Develop computational methods for predicting allosteric druggable sites.
problems.description: Allostery — Monod's 'second secret of life' — offers drug targets distinct from orthosteric sites. Cryptic allosteric sites open and close transiently, making prediction difficult. Mechanistic understanding of allosteric signaling remains incomplete.
problems.abstract: This project pursues allosteric drug design via (1) enhanced-sampling MD for cryptic pocket detection, (2) Markov state modeling of allosteric signaling pathways, and (3) ML-based prediction from sequence and structure. Progress opens new therapeutic targets.

problems.title: Oxygen-Tolerant Controlled Radical Polymerization
problems.tagline: Perform living radical polymerization in the presence of oxygen.
problems.summary: Enable industrial-scale ATRP/RAFT in ambient atmosphere.
problems.description: ATRP, RAFT, and NMP all suffer oxygen inhibition. PET-RAFT partially solves this via photoredox. Industrial-scale oxygen-tolerant systems would dramatically simplify precision polymer manufacturing.
problems.abstract: We target oxygen-tolerant CRP via (1) enzyme-based oxygen scavenging integrated with ATRP, (2) photoredox catalysts robust to O₂, and (3) continuous-flow systems minimizing exposure. Commercial-grade tolerance would broaden CRP deployment.

problems.title: Heaviest Stable Element
problems.tagline: Reach the island of nuclear stability near Z=120.
problems.summary: Synthesize and characterize elements in the predicted island of stability.
problems.description: Oganesson (Z=118) has millisecond half-life. The island of stability predicted near Z=120-126 could host relatively stable superheavy nuclei. Synthesis requires novel target-beam combinations and sensitive detection.
problems.abstract: This project pursues superheavy elements via (1) novel neutron-rich beam development, (2) sensitive detection of rare decay chains, and (3) predicted chemistry of superheavy elements in their position on the periodic table. Reaching the island of stability is a landmark in nuclear science.

problems.title: Understanding Tribochemistry
problems.tagline: Predict chemistry under mechanical force.
problems.summary: Develop predictive framework for force-induced chemical reactions.
problems.description: Mechanochemistry rules are emerging — force aligns reaction coordinates, enables otherwise forbidden pathways. Prediction of shear-induced reactions remains primitive. Applications span tribology, mechanosynthesis, and materials failure.
problems.abstract: We pursue tribochemistry theory via (1) constrained geometry ab initio MD under applied force, (2) experimental single-molecule force spectroscopy, and (3) ML models for force-strain-reactivity relationships. Progress enables mechanochemical design.

problems.title: Carbon Nanotube Chirality Control
problems.tagline: Synthesize single-chirality carbon nanotubes at scale.
problems.summary: Achieve scalable single-chirality carbon nanotube synthesis.
problems.description: Single-chirality CNT synthesis has been demonstrated for specific (n,m) indices. General and scalable chirality control remains a challenge. Applications span electronics, optoelectronics, and structural composites.
problems.abstract: This project targets CNT chirality control via (1) catalyst face-selective growth mechanisms, (2) organic template-directed synthesis of specific (n,m) types, and (3) post-synthesis separation via DNA wrapping. Scalable monodispersity unlocks CNT applications.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of Nucleation
problems.tagline: Provide a general theory of nucleation beyond the classical model.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theory of nucleation including non-classical pathways.
problems.description: Classical nucleation theory fails for many systems exhibiting two-step nucleation and prenucleation clusters. A general theory is lacking. Progress impacts crystallization, cloud formation, and materials synthesis.
problems.abstract: We pursue unified nucleation theory via (1) ab initio MD of early cluster formation in aqueous solutions, (2) cryo-EM characterization of prenucleation species, and (3) theoretical framework merging classical and non-classical pathways. Progress spans many applied fields.

problems.title: Biomimetic Mineralization Control
problems.tagline: Synthesize hierarchical biominerals with precision of nacre or bone.
problems.summary: Achieve synthetic control of biomineral polymorph, orientation, and hierarchy.
problems.description: Biological systems control crystal polymorph, orientation, and hierarchical structure with exquisite precision (nacre, bone, shells). Synthetic replication remains primitive. Success would enable tough, lightweight structural materials.
problems.abstract: This project pursues biomimetic mineralization via (1) protein- and peptide-directed crystal growth, (2) hierarchical template design from nano- to macroscale, and (3) bioinspired composite synthesis. Progress transforms structural materials design.

problems.title: General Late-Stage Functionalization
problems.tagline: Modify complex molecules at any chosen C-H bond.
problems.summary: Achieve general site-selective late-stage C-H functionalization.
problems.description: Late-stage functionalization modifies complex molecules at specific C-H bonds, accelerating drug discovery. Selectivity across diverse substrates remains challenging. Radical and photoredox approaches show promise.
problems.abstract: We target general late-stage functionalization via (1) steric-electronic radical selectivity models, (2) machine-learning prediction of C-H reactivity, and (3) orthogonal photoredox/metal catalysis. Progress transforms complex molecule modification.

problems.title: Quantum Computing for Chemistry
problems.tagline: Achieve quantum advantage on chemically meaningful problems.
problems.summary: Demonstrate quantum advantage over classical methods for real chemistry problems.
problems.description: Variational Quantum Eigensolvers (VQE) have solved small molecules on NISQ devices. Useful advantage over classical coupled-cluster and DMRG remains elusive, requiring 100+ logical qubits with error correction. The path is clear but long.
problems.abstract: This project pursues quantum chemistry via (1) improved ansätze for near-term VQE with noise mitigation, (2) resource estimation for fault-tolerant quantum chemistry, and (3) benchmark problems establishing quantum advantage. Progress positions quantum computing for chemistry applications.

problems.title: Complete Combustion Chemistry
problems.tagline: Predict combustion of arbitrary fuels from first principles.
problems.summary: Develop predictive combustion models for novel fuels.
problems.description: Gasoline combustion involves >1000 species and >10,000 reactions. Soot formation and pollutant chemistry depend on subtle kinetic details. Accurate models for novel biofuels and synthetic fuels require systematic extension.
problems.abstract: We pursue combustion chemistry via (1) automated reaction mechanism generation with ab initio rate calculations, (2) high-throughput kinetic experiments constraining key pathways, and (3) soot formation models benchmarked against shock tube data. Progress enables clean fuel design.

problems.title: Designer Enzymes for Non-Natural Reactions
problems.tagline: Design enzymes catalyzing reactions absent in nature.
problems.summary: Engineer enzymes for non-natural carbene/nitrene transfer and beyond.
problems.description: Directed evolution (Arnold, Nobel 2018) and de novo enzyme design (Baker lab) have yielded enzymes for carbene and nitrene transfer. Expansion to arbitrary non-natural reactions awaits better scaffold libraries and computational design tools.
problems.abstract: This project targets designer enzymes via (1) RoseTTAFold-guided active site engineering, (2) directed evolution under high-throughput screening, and (3) combining catalytic scaffolds with non-natural cofactors. Progress enables biocatalytic synthesis of arbitrary small molecules.

problems.title: Multicomponent Reaction Design
problems.tagline: Design new multicomponent reactions systematically.
problems.summary: Develop principles for rational multicomponent reaction discovery.
problems.description: Ugi, Passerini, and Biginelli reactions exemplify multicomponent reactions. Systematic design principles for new MCRs are missing. These reactions offer efficient access to complex molecular diversity.
problems.abstract: We target MCR design via (1) ML models trained on reaction databases for compatibility prediction, (2) high-throughput experimentation validating candidate combinations, and (3) mechanism-guided rational design. Progress enables diversity-oriented synthesis.

problems.title: Accurate Excited-State Chemistry Prediction
problems.tagline: Predict photochemistry with quantitative accuracy.
problems.summary: Achieve reliable computational prediction of excited-state dynamics.
problems.description: TD-DFT and CASPT2 provide partial excited-state accuracy but fail at conical intersections and for charge-transfer states. Non-adiabatic dynamics at scale is computationally expensive. Progress enables photocatalyst and photosensitizer design.
problems.abstract: This project pursues excited-state chemistry via (1) ML-corrected TD-DFT for charge-transfer accuracy, (2) scalable non-adiabatic MD with surface hopping, and (3) benchmark datasets for photochemical reactivity. Progress enables photocatalysis design.

problems.title: Programmable Drug Delivery
problems.tagline: Drug carriers with in vivo feedback-controlled release.
problems.summary: Develop drug delivery systems with logical in vivo response.
problems.description: pH-, temperature-, and enzyme-responsive delivery systems are known. True programmability — logic gates, feedback control, and autonomous decision-making in vivo — remains a frontier. Progress enables personalized medicine.
problems.abstract: We target programmable drug delivery via (1) molecular logic gates responsive to multiple biomarkers, (2) feedback-controlled release tied to local biochemistry, and (3) biocompatible materials enabling long-term operation. Progress transforms disease treatment.

problems.title: Exact Schrödinger for Large Molecules
problems.tagline: Solve the electronic Schrödinger equation exactly beyond small molecules.
problems.summary: Extend exact quantum chemistry methods beyond ~20-electron limits.
problems.description: Full configuration interaction scales exponentially, limiting exact solutions to ~20 electrons. DMRG, QMC, and coupled cluster offer approximations with varying accuracy. Pushing the exact frontier beyond current limits is a central computational chemistry problem.
problems.abstract: This project pursues scalable exact methods via (1) adaptive-selected CI with ML pruning, (2) parallel DMRG for large active spaces, and (3) quantum computing as a long-term complement. Progress enables exact treatment of correlated systems.

problems.title: Abiogenesis: Prebiotic Chemistry to Life
problems.tagline: Bridge prebiotic chemistry to self-replicating systems.
problems.summary: Establish chemical pathways from simple molecules to minimal life.
problems.description: Miller-Urey, RNA world hypothesis, and lipid vesicle formation provide partial pictures. Linking these to self-replication and evolution remains a central open problem. This intersects chemistry, biology, and Earth sciences.
problems.abstract: We pursue abiogenesis via (1) wet-dry cycle experiments producing RNA-like polymers from prebiotic monomers, (2) synthesis of minimal protocells with metabolism and genetics, and (3) evolutionary simulations of emerging chemical networks. Progress illuminates life's origin.

problems.title: First-Principles Catalyst Design
problems.tagline: Rationally design a catalyst before synthesis.
problems.summary: Predict optimal catalyst structures from first principles.
problems.description: The Sabatier principle provides a qualitative guide. Descriptor-based screening (d-band center, scaling relations) accelerates discovery but true rational prediction remains elusive. Progress enables targeted catalyst development.
problems.abstract: This project pursues first-principles catalyst design via (1) high-throughput DFT screening over composition-structure space, (2) ML surrogates trained on benchmark catalysis databases, and (3) experimental validation cycles. Progress transforms catalyst discovery.

problems.title: Programmable Matter
problems.tagline: Materials that reconfigure shape and function on command.
problems.summary: Develop materials with arbitrary programmable shape and function changes.
problems.description: Shape-memory polymers and responsive hydrogels provide limited programmability. True programmable matter — arbitrary shape, modulus, and function changes — remains aspirational. Progress impacts robotics, biomedicine, and construction.
problems.abstract: We pursue programmable matter via (1) multi-responsive polymer networks with orthogonal triggers, (2) modular robotic elements self-assembling into targeted structures, and (3) hierarchical design for macroscopic response from molecular changes. Progress transforms adaptive materials.

problems.title: Chirality Amplification
problems.tagline: Understand how tiny enantiomeric excesses amplify to homochirality.
problems.summary: Explain chirality amplification mechanisms from small to near-perfect ee.
problems.description: The Soai reaction demonstrates autocatalytic amplification. Viedma ripening shows crystal-mediated amplification. How biological homochirality arose from presumed small initial imbalances remains debated.
problems.abstract: This project targets chirality amplification via (1) detailed kinetics of autocatalytic Soai-type reactions, (2) mechanistic studies of Viedma ripening in diverse systems, and (3) models linking prebiotic conditions to observed biological homochirality. Progress connects chemistry to life's origin.

problems.title: Universal Solvent-Free Synthesis
problems.tagline: Eliminate solvents from synthetic chemistry.
problems.summary: Develop universally applicable solvent-free synthetic methods.
problems.description: Mechanochemistry (ball milling) enables solvent-free reactions but is limited in substrate scope. Most organic chemistry still requires solvents. Universal solvent-free synthesis would dramatically improve sustainability.
problems.abstract: We target solvent-free synthesis via (1) systematic mechanochemistry exploration across reaction classes, (2) solid-state reactions enabled by co-crystal engineering, and (3) continuous-flow solid-state processes for scale-up. Progress transforms green chemistry.

problems.title: Chemically Autonomous Artificial Cell
problems.tagline: Build a self-sustaining chemical system that grows and divides.
problems.summary: Construct a minimal artificial cell with autonomous metabolism and reproduction.
problems.description: Protocells integrating lipid vesicles, RNA, and metabolism have been demonstrated in parts. A fully self-sustaining chemical system that grows and divides remains out of reach. This defines the chemistry-biology boundary.
problems.abstract: This project pursues artificial cells via (1) minimal metabolic networks coupled to lipid synthesis, (2) RNA-based genetic systems for heritable variation, and (3) integration into dividing protocells. Progress bridges chemistry and biology.

problems.title: Accurate Force Fields for All Elements
problems.tagline: Universal classical force fields matching quantum accuracy.
problems.summary: Develop force fields accurate across the full periodic table.
problems.description: AMBER, CHARMM, and OPLS are well-developed for biomolecules. Small-molecule and transition-metal force fields remain approximate. Machine-learned potentials (ANI, MACE) are rapidly closing the gap for diverse systems.
problems.abstract: We pursue universal force fields via (1) ML potentials trained on DFT across the periodic table, (2) transferable functional forms capturing long-range effects, and (3) benchmark validation across materials classes. Progress enables accurate classical simulation.

problems.title: Complete Heterogeneous Catalysis Understanding
problems.tagline: Explain and predict heterogeneous catalysis atomistically.
problems.summary: Achieve atomistic understanding of heterogeneous catalysis active sites and dynamics.
problems.description: Surface science and operando spectroscopy have illuminated active sites, support effects, and dynamic restructuring. Heterogeneous catalysis design remains largely empirical. Atomistic prediction would transform catalyst development.
problems.abstract: This project pursues heterogeneous catalysis via (1) operando characterization of dynamic active sites under reaction conditions, (2) ab initio kinetic Monte Carlo simulations of full catalytic cycles, and (3) inverse design of structural motifs. Progress enables rational catalyst engineering.

problems.title: Chemical Evolution Theory
problems.tagline: Explain how chemical complexity increases spontaneously.
problems.summary: Develop a theoretical framework for chemical system complexity growth.
problems.description: Autocatalytic sets (Kauffman, Hordijk) suggest how complexity might emerge. What drives chemical evolution and complexity increase remains unclear. A predictive theory would bridge chemistry and biology.
problems.abstract: We pursue chemical evolution theory via (1) large-scale reaction network simulations seeded with autocatalytic motifs, (2) entropy-based measures of chemical complexity, and (3) experimental systems demonstrating spontaneous complexity growth. Progress addresses life's emergence.

problems.title: Inverse Materials Design
problems.tagline: Given target properties, predict composition and structure.
problems.summary: Achieve reliable inverse materials design from desired properties.
problems.description: Forward prediction (composition → properties) has improved dramatically with ML. Inverse design (properties → composition) is the harder problem, requiring multi-objective optimization and synthesizability prediction. Progress transforms materials discovery.
problems.abstract: This project targets inverse materials design via (1) generative models (VAE, diffusion) sampling composition-structure space, (2) multi-objective Bayesian optimization with synthesizability constraints, and (3) experimental validation cycles. Progress accelerates materials innovation.

problems.title: Mastering Supramolecular Polymerization
problems.tagline: Achieve sequence-controlled living supramolecular polymerization.
problems.summary: Develop living supramolecular polymerization with full sequence control.
problems.description: Pathway complexity and kinetic traps challenge supramolecular polymerization. Living supramolecular polymerization is emerging. Sequence control analogous to covalent polymers remains a frontier.
problems.abstract: We pursue supramolecular polymerization control via (1) seeded living polymerization protocols, (2) orthogonal monomer assembly for sequence control, and (3) kinetic trap avoidance strategies. Progress enables new classes of dynamic materials.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of Interfaces
problems.tagline: Atomistic description of all interface types.
problems.summary: Develop comprehensive atomistic understanding of solid-liquid, liquid-liquid, and solid-gas interfaces.
problems.description: Interfaces govern electrochemistry, heterogeneous catalysis, and biology. Operando characterization (SFG, X-ray reflectivity) is advancing. A unified atomistic framework is missing.
problems.abstract: This project pursues interface science via (1) SFG and X-ray studies under operando conditions, (2) ab initio MD of full interfacial regions, and (3) unified theoretical frameworks across interface types. Progress impacts many applied chemistries.

problems.title: Artificial Molecular Evolution
problems.tagline: Evolve arbitrary (non-nucleic-acid) molecules under selection.
problems.summary: Extend SELEX-style evolution to non-nucleic-acid molecule classes.
problems.description: SELEX allows evolution of nucleic acid aptamers. Extending evolution to arbitrary polymer classes (peptoids, XNAs) requires new selection and amplification mechanisms. Progress enables tailored function in synthetic polymers.
problems.abstract: We pursue artificial molecular evolution via (1) XNA polymerases with efficient templated synthesis, (2) peptoid libraries and selection schemes, and (3) in vitro evolution cycles producing functional non-natural biomolecules. Progress expands evolvable chemistry.

problems.title: Predictive Toxicology
problems.tagline: Predict toxicity from chemical structure reliably.
problems.summary: Achieve reliable computational toxicity prediction across novel scaffolds.
problems.description: QSAR models improve for known scaffolds but fail for novel chemotypes. Organ-on-chip platforms and ML toxicogenomics provide complementary data. Fully computational toxicity prediction remains distant.
problems.abstract: This project targets predictive toxicology via (1) deep learning models trained on comprehensive toxicogenomics, (2) organ-on-chip validation for key assays, and (3) uncertainty quantification for novel scaffolds. Progress reduces drug attrition and animal testing.

problems.title: Scalable Defect-Free Quantum Dot Synthesis
problems.tagline: Make defect-free quantum dots for every wavelength at scale.
problems.summary: Achieve scalable synthesis of narrow-emission quantum dots across the full visible spectrum.
problems.description: CdSe quantum dots are well-developed. Cd-free InP dots are catching up. Defect-free, narrow emission across all wavelengths at commercial scale remains challenging. Applications span displays, imaging, and solar cells.
problems.abstract: We pursue scalable quantum dot synthesis via (1) continuous-flow reactors for uniform nucleation-growth, (2) surface chemistry suppressing defects in InP dots, and (3) post-synthesis purification for monodispersity. Progress enables commercial quantum dot applications.

problems.title: Complete Reaction Mechanism Database
problems.tagline: Compile and computationally validate all known reaction mechanisms.
problems.summary: Create comprehensive database of verified reaction mechanisms with computational support.
problems.description: Thousands of reaction mechanisms are known individually but no comprehensive database with computational validation exists. Such a resource would accelerate mechanism-based synthesis design and education.
problems.abstract: This project creates a reaction mechanism database via (1) curation and annotation of published mechanisms, (2) DFT benchmarking of key transition states, and (3) integration with retrosynthesis AI tools. Progress creates a foundational resource for chemistry.

problems.title: Self-Replicating Chemical Systems
problems.tagline: Build chemical systems with exponential replication.
problems.summary: Achieve template-directed exponential self-replication in synthetic chemistry.
problems.description: Von Kiedrowski's 1986 minimal replicator demonstrated template-directed synthesis. Exponential (vs parabolic) replication is rare. Robust self-replicating systems remain key targets of systems chemistry.
problems.abstract: We pursue self-replicating systems via (1) cross-catalytic replicator networks, (2) nucleic acid analogs supporting template replication, and (3) hybrid peptide-nucleic acid replicators. Progress sheds light on life's emergence.

problems.title: Hierarchical Self-Assembly Control
problems.tagline: Control self-assembly at every hierarchical level like proteins.
problems.summary: Achieve biology-level control of hierarchical self-assembly in synthetic systems.
problems.description: Biological proteins achieve exquisite hierarchical structure (primary → quaternary). Synthetic systems demonstrate limited hierarchical control. Multi-level programming of self-assembly remains a frontier.
problems.abstract: This project pursues hierarchical self-assembly via (1) orthogonal interaction design across hierarchy levels, (2) programmable folding in synthetic polymers, and (3) assembly cascades mimicking protein biogenesis. Progress bridges synthetic and biological assembly.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Chemical Bonding
problems.tagline: Unify all models of chemical bonding.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theoretical framework covering all chemical bond types.
problems.description: Lewis, valence bond, and molecular orbital theories coexist with different utilities. The definition of 'chemical bond' is still debated. Charge-shift bonds, multi-center bonds, and agostic interactions resist simple classification.
problems.abstract: We pursue unified bonding theory via (1) quantum-theory-of-atoms-in-molecules analysis across bond types, (2) energy decomposition unifying different bonding pictures, and (3) machine learning identifying new bonding patterns. Progress clarifies chemistry's foundational concepts.

problems.title: Permanent Carbon Sequestration
problems.tagline: Lock away captured CO₂ for geological timescales.
problems.summary: Develop gigatonne-scale economically viable permanent CO₂ sequestration.
problems.description: Mineralization, ocean storage, and geological sequestration offer permanence options. Scaling to gigatonne/year with centuries-scale stability and economic viability is an open challenge. Progress is critical to climate goals.
problems.abstract: This project pursues permanent sequestration via (1) basalt mineralization at scale (CarbFix model), (2) ocean alkalinity enhancement, and (3) techno-economic analysis of integrated systems. Progress enables climate-relevant deployment.

problems.title: Ab Initio Reaction Rate Prediction
problems.tagline: Predict reaction rates to factor-of-10 accuracy routinely.
problems.summary: Achieve routine ab initio reaction rate prediction within factor of 10.
problems.description: Transition state theory with tunneling corrections provides rate predictions. Kinetics is harder than thermodynamics — factor-of-10 accuracy is rarely achieved. Progress enables mechanism-based catalyst and reactor design.
problems.abstract: We target ab initio rate prediction via (1) high-accuracy coupled cluster transition state calculations, (2) quantum tunneling corrections via instanton methods, and (3) ML-calibrated error estimates. Progress enables reliable kinetic modeling.

problems.title: Atomic-Scale Electrochemistry
problems.tagline: Atomically resolve electrode-electrolyte interface dynamics.
problems.summary: Achieve complete atomic-scale understanding of electrochemical interfaces.
problems.description: The electrode-electrolyte interface controls electrochemistry but is difficult to characterize at atomic scale. Grand canonical DFT is developing to match constant-potential conditions. Progress enables rational electrocatalyst design.
problems.abstract: This project pursues atomic-scale electrochemistry via (1) grand canonical DFT simulations of interfaces under potential control, (2) operando X-ray and STM characterization, and (3) atomistic mechanism elucidation for key electrocatalytic reactions. Progress transforms electrocatalysis.

problems.title: Chemical Information Storage Beyond DNA
problems.tagline: Store information densely in non-biological molecules.
problems.summary: Develop chemical information storage beyond nucleic acids.
problems.description: DNA data storage is well-demonstrated. Non-biological molecular information storage (sequence-defined polymers, MOF libraries) could offer alternatives with different read/write properties.
problems.abstract: We pursue non-DNA molecular information storage via (1) sequence-defined synthetic polymers, (2) MS-based readout protocols, and (3) benchmarking vs DNA storage metrics. Progress expands information storage options.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Chemical Reactivity
problems.tagline: One theory to predict all chemical reactions.
problems.summary: Develop a universal predictive theory of chemical reactivity.
problems.description: Woodward-Hoffmann rules, FMO theory, and Marcus theory offer partial predictive power. A universal theory capable of predicting all reactions remains elusive. Machine learning may provide a data-driven surrogate.
problems.abstract: This project pursues unified reactivity theory via (1) foundation models trained on chemical reaction data, (2) integration of mechanistic rules into ML architectures, and (3) interpretable models revealing universal principles. Progress closes a century-old gap.

problems.title: Why These 92 Natural Elements?
problems.tagline: Why do 92 elements exist rather than some other number?
problems.summary: Explain the observed set of naturally occurring elements from nuclear physics.
problems.description: Nuclear physics determines element stability, but why the underlying force constants take their values is unknown. Anthropic reasoning, landscape arguments, and nuclear structure models all bear on this. Crosses into fundamental physics.
problems.abstract: This project examines element existence via (1) nuclear structure calculations across isotope chart, (2) anthropic analysis of constants' permissible ranges, and (3) connections to GUT proton decay predictions. Progress clarifies chemistry's physical foundations.

problems.title: Prediction of Emergent Properties
problems.tagline: Predict superconductivity, ferroelectricity from first principles.
problems.summary: Reliably predict emergent materials properties without prior examples.
problems.description: Superconductivity, ferroelectricity, and catalytic activity are emergent collective phenomena not simply derivable from constituents. Prediction from first principles remains unreliable. Reductionism meets emergence.
problems.abstract: We address emergence prediction via (1) multi-scale theory bridging electronic to mesoscopic, (2) ML models trained on materials property databases, and (3) theoretical bounds on predictability. Progress frames the limits of reductionism.

problems.title: Chemical Complexity Theory
problems.tagline: Define what makes a molecule 'complex'.
problems.summary: Establish a universal measure of molecular complexity tied to function.
problems.description: Cronin's assembly index attempts to measure complexity via synthesis difficulty. No consensus metric exists. Relating complexity to function remains elusive. Touches on biosignature detection.
problems.abstract: This project pursues chemical complexity via (1) analysis of assembly index and alternative metrics, (2) connection to function through structure-activity data, and (3) applications to biosignature detection. Progress impacts astrobiology and theoretical chemistry.

problems.title: Origin of Biological Homochirality
problems.tagline: Why are biological amino acids L and sugars D?
problems.summary: Explain the origin of biological homochirality from prebiotic conditions.
problems.description: Biological life uses L-amino acids and D-sugars exclusively. Parity violation in the weak force, circularly polarized light, and statistical fluctuations are candidate explanations. Amplification mechanisms are debated.
problems.abstract: We pursue homochirality origins via (1) analysis of parity-violation contributions to amino acid stability, (2) prebiotic experiments under CP-light conditions, and (3) amplification mechanisms tied to crystallization. Progress addresses a central origin-of-life question.

problems.title: Water at Interfaces
problems.tagline: Structure and dynamics of water at biological and material surfaces.
problems.summary: Resolve the structure and dynamics of interfacial water molecularly.
problems.description: Interfacial water governs hydrophobic effect, enzyme catalysis, and membrane dynamics. Decades of debate persist on its structure. Key to biology and catalysis. This is distinct from bulk water understanding (#13).
problems.abstract: This project targets interfacial water via (1) operando SFG measurements across diverse interfaces, (2) ab initio MD simulations including nuclear quantum effects, and (3) theoretical frameworks for hydrophobic effects. Progress impacts biology and materials.

problems.title: Chemistry Reduced to Physics
problems.tagline: Is chemistry fundamentally derivable from physics?
problems.summary: Address whether chemistry reduces to quantum mechanics.
problems.description: Dirac (1929) noted that the equations of chemistry are known but too complex to solve. Whether chemistry is genuinely emergent or merely computationally complex is debated. Downward causation questions challenge strict reductionism.
problems.abstract: This project examines chemistry-physics reduction by (1) analyzing emergent chemical concepts (bond, molecule) in quantum frameworks, (2) case studies of prediction successes and failures, and (3) philosophical analysis of reductionism. Progress clarifies chemistry's ontological status.

problems.title: Materials Prediction Before Synthesis
problems.tagline: Predict material properties before synthesis attempts.
problems.summary: Reliably predict synthesizability and all target properties before making materials.
problems.description: Inverse design dreams of predicting materials before synthesis. Synthesizability itself is unpredicted. Multi-property optimization with synthesizability constraints remains unsolved. Crosses materials science and chemistry.
problems.abstract: We pursue pre-synthesis prediction via (1) ML synthesizability scoring tied to experimental databases, (2) multi-property Bayesian optimization, and (3) active learning integration with high-throughput experimentation. Progress accelerates materials discovery.

problems.title: Universal Definition of Aromaticity
problems.tagline: A unified metric for aromaticity across all systems.
problems.summary: Establish a universal aromaticity metric applicable to all chemical systems.
problems.description: Hückel (4n+2) rule works for planar monocycles. 3D aromaticity, σ-aromaticity, and Möbius aromaticity require separate treatments. A unified metric capturing aromaticity in all systems is lacking.
problems.abstract: This project pursues aromaticity theory via (1) systematic comparison of NICS, HOMA, and magnetic criteria across systems, (2) quantum chemical topology analysis, and (3) ML-identified structural signatures of aromaticity. Progress unifies a central chemistry concept.

problems.title: Complete Chemical Space Exploration
problems.tagline: Meaningfully explore the 10⁶⁰ possible drug-like molecules.
problems.summary: Develop AI for meaningful exploration of the vast chemical space.
problems.description: Drug-like chemical space contains ~10⁶⁰ molecules. Current exploration samples an infinitesimal fraction. Whether AI can explore meaningfully remains an open question. Crosses computational and medicinal chemistry.
problems.abstract: We pursue chemical space exploration via (1) generative models sampling biased toward drug-likeness, (2) active learning integrated with synthesis and assays, and (3) theoretical bounds on meaningful coverage. Progress transforms drug discovery.

problems.title: Self-Aware Chemical Systems
problems.tagline: Chemical systems that model themselves.
problems.summary: Develop chemical systems exhibiting self-modeling or cognition.
problems.description: Chemical cognition is highly speculative. Self-modeling chemical systems would represent a new class of artificial life. No clear experimental pathway exists.
problems.abstract: This project examines chemical cognition via (1) information-theoretic analysis of chemical networks, (2) design of systems with feedback approximating self-modeling, and (3) philosophical analysis of required criteria. Progress frames chemistry's relation to cognition.

problems.title: Formalizing Chemical Intuition
problems.tagline: Capture how expert chemists 'see' reactivity.
problems.summary: Formalize expert chemical intuition for machine learning systems.
problems.description: Expert chemists make rapid reactivity predictions based on tacit knowledge. Neural networks reproduce some intuitions but not all. Formalization would enable AI-assisted chemistry education and discovery.
problems.abstract: We pursue chemical intuition formalization via (1) interviews and structured elicitation of expert knowledge, (2) neural network analysis of learned representations, and (3) hybrid symbolic-neural models. Progress advances chemistry education and AI.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Solution Chemistry
problems.tagline: Unified theory for concentrated and mixed-solvent systems.
problems.summary: Develop a predictive theory of solution chemistry beyond dilute limits.
problems.description: Debye-Hückel works for dilute electrolytes. Concentrated solutions, mixed solvents, and ionic liquids lack a general theory. Progress enables predictive chemistry in real-world conditions.
problems.abstract: This project pursues solution chemistry theory via (1) ab initio MD in concentrated electrolytes, (2) integral equation theories for correlations, and (3) benchmark datasets across non-ideal conditions. Progress transforms industrial chemistry modeling.

problems.title: Chemistry in Extreme Environments
problems.tagline: Predict chemistry under neutron star or gas giant conditions.
problems.summary: Characterize chemistry under extreme pressure, temperature, and radiation.
problems.description: Gas giant interiors, neutron star surfaces, and interstellar medium subject chemistry to extreme conditions far from terrestrial experience. Experimental access is limited; theoretical prediction is the primary tool.
problems.abstract: We pursue extreme environment chemistry via (1) high-pressure DFT for planetary interiors, (2) diamond anvil cell experiments benchmarking theory, and (3) astrochemistry models for interstellar reactions. Progress connects chemistry to astrophysics.

problems.title: Molecular Machines Doing Macroscopic Work
problems.tagline: Collective action of molecular machines producing useful macroscopic output.
problems.summary: Achieve macroscopic useful work from collective molecular machine arrays.
problems.description: Individual molecular motors and Brownian ratchets are demonstrated. Collective action of trillions producing macroscopic work requires orchestration mechanisms absent in current systems. Represents nanotechnology's most ambitious goal.
problems.abstract: This project pursues macroscopic molecular work via (1) hierarchical assembly of motor arrays, (2) chemical fuel distribution networks, and (3) mechanical amplification designs. Progress realizes nanotechnology's central vision.

problems.title: Complete Catalysis Selectivity Understanding
problems.tagline: Predict chemo-, regio-, stereo-, and site-selectivity simultaneously.
problems.summary: Achieve simultaneous predictive control of all selectivity types in catalysis.
problems.description: Chemo-, regio-, stereo-, and site-selectivity each admit partial prediction. Simultaneous control across new substrates remains impossible. Progress would transform synthetic methodology.
problems.abstract: We pursue complete selectivity prediction via (1) high-accuracy DFT on competing transition states, (2) ML-based selectivity models trained on curated datasets, and (3) automated high-throughput screening closing the loop. Progress transforms rational catalysis.

problems.title: Chemistry of Primordial Earth
problems.tagline: What was the atmosphere and chemistry of early Earth?
problems.summary: Reconstruct the chemistry of Earth's early atmosphere and hydrothermal environments.
problems.description: Earth's early atmosphere — reducing vs CO₂-rich — affects all origin-of-life models. Hydrothermal vent chemistry vs surface chemistry is debated. Geochemical evidence is sparse and indirect.
problems.abstract: This project reconstructs primordial Earth chemistry via (1) laboratory simulation of candidate atmospheric compositions, (2) isotope-based constraints from oldest rocks, and (3) hydrothermal vent analog experiments. Progress anchors origin-of-life research.

problems.title: General Theory of Chemical Self-Organization
problems.tagline: Predictive theory of chemical self-organization.
problems.summary: Develop a predictive framework for chemical self-organization in novel systems.
problems.description: Bénard cells, Belousov-Zhabotinsky reactions, and Turing patterns illustrate chemical self-organization. Prigogine's framework captures general principles but predictive power for new systems is limited.
problems.abstract: We pursue self-organization theory via (1) simulation of reaction-diffusion systems across parameter spaces, (2) linear stability analysis generalized to complex networks, and (3) experimental validation in microfluidic systems. Progress generalizes chemistry's classical self-organization results.

problems.title: True Molecular Computing
problems.tagline: Build Turing-complete chemical reaction networks with practical utility.
problems.summary: Develop Turing-complete chemical computing systems with practical applications.
problems.description: DNA computing and chemical reaction network computing have demonstrated logic gates. Turing-complete systems with practical utility remain out of reach. Challenges include speed, scalability, and readout.
problems.abstract: This project pursues molecular computing via (1) scalable DNA-based circuits, (2) CRN-based neural networks, and (3) interfaces to electronic readout. Progress enables in vivo diagnostics and beyond.

problems.title: Predicting Properties Not Yet Imagined
problems.tagline: Discover material properties we haven't conceived.
problems.summary: Develop methodology for discovering unexpected material properties.
problems.description: Inverse design assumes known property targets. Discovering entirely new material properties requires a different framework. Serendipity is not scalable; systematic exploration is needed.
problems.abstract: We pursue property discovery via (1) broad multi-property characterization of novel materials, (2) ML clustering for unusual behavior, and (3) theoretical frameworks for property anticipation. Progress expands the space of materials innovation.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Solid-State Bonding
problems.tagline: Unify bonding descriptions for extended solid systems.
problems.summary: Develop unified chemical bonding theory for solids including topological aspects.
problems.description: Bands, Wannier functions, and topological insulators highlight solid-state bonding's richness. Chemical bonding descriptions in extended systems are still evolving. Connections to molecular concepts are not fully established.
problems.abstract: This project pursues solid-state bonding theory via (1) Wannier analysis across material classes, (2) chemical-bond-aware topological invariants, and (3) unified framework connecting molecular and solid bonding. Progress clarifies solid-state chemistry.

problems.title: Dark Chemistry
problems.tagline: Identify reactions whose products escape detection.
problems.summary: Develop methods for detecting 'invisible' reactions missed by current instruments.
problems.description: Reactions may occur whose products are undetectable by standard techniques. What chemistry is hidden? Novel analytical techniques and computational prediction may reveal dark reactions.
problems.abstract: We pursue dark chemistry detection via (1) ultrasensitive analytical methods, (2) computational prediction of unstudied reaction products, and (3) isotope labeling strategies revealing hidden pathways. Progress expands known chemistry.

problems.title: Chemistry's Role in Consciousness
problems.tagline: Bridge neurotransmitter chemistry to subjective experience.
problems.summary: Address the explanatory gap between neurochemistry and consciousness.
problems.description: Neurotransmitters, ion channels, and synaptic chemistry underlie brain function. How this chemistry gives rise to consciousness remains an explanatory gap. This intersects chemistry, neuroscience, and philosophy of mind.
problems.abstract: This project examines chemistry-consciousness via (1) detailed neurotransmitter-function maps, (2) integrated information analysis at neurochemical level, and (3) philosophical analysis of the explanatory gap. Progress touches a deep philosophical question.

problems.title: Artificial Ecosystem Chemistry
problems.tagline: Closed-loop chemical cycles mimicking planetary biogeochemistry.
problems.summary: Design self-regulating artificial ecosystems at planetary relevance.
problems.description: Earth's biogeochemistry is self-regulating at planetary scale. Synthetic closed-loop systems (Biosphere 2) have failed to replicate this. Understanding the principles is key to space colonization and sustainable industry.
problems.abstract: We pursue artificial ecosystem chemistry via (1) minimal closed-loop systems with elemental cycling, (2) network theory of biogeochemical feedbacks, and (3) scale-up experiments building from microcosms. Progress enables sustainable design.

problems.title: Predicting Scientific Discovery
problems.tagline: Can AI predict the next breakthrough?
problems.summary: Develop methods for predicting scientific discoveries before they happen.
problems.description: Literature-based discovery (Swanson) and AI hypothesis generation attempt to predict discoveries. Whether creative leaps are formalizable is debated. Progress would reshape research strategy and funding.
problems.abstract: This project examines discovery prediction via (1) large language models trained on scientific literature, (2) graph-based analysis of concept co-occurrence, and (3) retrospective validation against known discoveries. Progress frames the limits of AI-driven science.

problems.title: Unifying Organic, Inorganic, and Biological Chemistry
problems.tagline: Bridge the three cultures of chemistry into a common language.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theoretical framework spanning chemistry's traditional divisions.
problems.description: Organic, inorganic, and biological chemistry have distinct concepts, methods, and communities. Systems chemistry attempts a bridge. A common theoretical language unifying all three would transform chemistry.
problems.abstract: This project pursues chemistry unification via (1) systems chemistry approaches linking organic and biological, (2) inorganic-organic hybrid frameworks (coordination chemistry, organometallics), and (3) theoretical frameworks spanning all three. Progress would unify chemistry.
